rationale: The cited paper is not just listed in passing; it is specifically summarized in the theory section as evidence that marketing effects in pharmaceuticals vary over the life cycle, and it helps motivate H1 about declining transient effects over time. In the conclusion, the citing paper again uses it as a direct empirical benchmark, stating its transient-effect pattern is consistent with Narayanan et al. (2005). This indicates a meaningful framing and positioning role tied to hypothesis development and interpretation of results.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Narayanan et al. (2005) as prior evidence on time-varying transient marketing communication effects in pharmaceuticals, then extends that line by jointly modeling how both transient and persistent effects evolve after launch. It uses Narayanan et al. as a comparison point for the transient-pattern findings.
